title: epipactis persica subsp. danubialis
intro: Epipactis persica subsp. danubialis, also known as the Danube helleborine, is a subspecies of the Epipactis persica orchid native to Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of iridoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Epipactis persica subsp. danubialis include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are typically used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
